topics.meaning: Transport and communication are the lifelines of the national economy because they move goods, people and information.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Today the world has been converted into a large village with the help of efficient and fast moving means of transport. Transport and communication are vital because production is not complete until goods reach the actual consumers, and the movement of goods and services from supply to demand locations requires transport. The pace of development of a country depends upon the production of goods and services as well as their movement over space, so efficient means of transport are prerequisites for the fast development of the country. For a long time trade and transport were restricted to a limited space, but with developments in science and technology the area of influence of trade and transport expanded far and wide. Today our trade and economy depend heavily on transport, communication and trade, which together form the lifelines of the national economy. Communication, the transmission of information, is also a prerequisite for local, national and global trade.

topics.meaning: India has the second largest road network in the world, and roadways have several advantages over railways.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: India has the second largest road network in the world, aggregating to about 62.16 lakh km (2020-21), and roadways have preceded railways and still have an edge over them. The growing importance of road transport is because the construction cost of roads is much lower than railway lines, roads can traverse comparatively more dissected and undulating topography and higher gradients of slopes, road transport is economical for transporting fewer persons and smaller goods over short distances, it provides door-to-door service lowering loading and unloading costs, and it acts as a feeder to other modes like railways, air and sea ports. Roads in India are classified into six categories: Golden Quadrilateral Super Highways linking Delhi-Kolkata-Chennai-Mumbai and the North-South and East-West corridors; National Highways linking extreme parts of the country, maintained by the CPWD; State Highways linking state capitals with district headquarters; District Roads connecting district headquarters with other places; Other Roads or rural roads linking villages with towns; and Border Roads built along the borders for strategic importance.

topics.meaning: Railways are the principal mode of transport for freight and passengers in India and the backbone of the country's economy.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Railways are the principal mode of transportation for freight and passengers in India, and they make it possible to conduct multifarious activities like business, sightseeing, pilgrimage and the transportation of goods over longer distances. Apart from an important means of transport, the Indian Railways have been a great integrating force for more than 160 years, binding the economic life of the country and accelerating the development of industry and agriculture. The Indian Railways is the largest public sector undertaking in the country. The distribution pattern of the railway network in the country has been largely influenced by physiographic, economic and administrative factors; the northern plains with their vast level land, high population density and rich agricultural resources provided the most favourable condition for the growth of railways, while it was difficult to lay railway lines on sandy plains, on the Himalayas, and in hilly, swampy and forested regions. The Indian Railways is divided into a number of zones for administrative convenience.

topics.meaning: Pipelines, waterways and airways are important means of transport for liquids, bulky goods and fast long-distance travel.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Pipelines are a new arrival on the transportation map and are now used for transporting crude oil, petroleum products and natural gas from oilfields and refineries to consumers; solids can also be transported as slurry. Three important pipeline networks include the one from Mumbai High to Mumbai's refineries, the Salaya-Mathura pipeline, and the Hazira-Vijaipur-Jagdishpur pipeline carrying natural gas. Waterways are the cheapest means of transport, most suitable for carrying heavy and bulky goods, and are fuel-efficient and environment-friendly; India has inland navigation waterways and important National Waterways like the Ganga between Allahabad and Haldia (NW1) and the Brahmaputra between Sadiya and Dhubri (NW2). The major seaports include Kandla, Mumbai, Marmagao, New Mangalore, Kochi, Tuticorin, Chennai, Visakhapatnam, Paradip, Haldia and Kolkata. Airways are the fastest, most comfortable and prestigious mode of transport, able to cover difficult terrains like high mountains, deserts, dense forests and oceanic stretches; air transport was nationalised in 1953, and Pawanhans provides helicopter and aircraft services.
topics.easyExplanation: Pipelines carry oil and gas, waterways carry heavy goods cheaply, and airways are the fastest way to travel long distances over difficult land.

topics.meaning: India has a long coastline with major seaports that handle the bulk of the country's foreign trade.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: With a long coastline of about 7,516.6 km, India is dotted with major and intermediate ports that handle the bulk of the country's foreign trade. These major ports handle the largest share of India's overseas trade. On the western coast, Kandla in Kachchh was the first port developed soon after independence to ease the volume of trade on the Mumbai port and is a tidal port. Mumbai is the biggest port with a spacious natural and well-sheltered harbour, and the Jawaharlal Nehru port was planned to decongest the Mumbai port. Marmagao, New Mangalore and Kochi are other important western ports. On the eastern coast, Tuticorin, Chennai, Visakhapatnam (the deepest landlocked and well-protected port), Paradip and Haldia and Kolkata (a riverine port) handle trade. These ports support the import of crude oil, machinery and fertilizers and the export of iron ore, coffee, tea and manufactured goods, playing a vital role in international trade.

topics.meaning: Communication is the transmission of information, carried out through personal and mass means like the postal system, telephone, radio, television and the internet.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Communication has been an important aspect of human life, and India has one of the largest telecom networks in Asia. Communication is divided into personal and mass communication. Personal communication includes the postal network, which is the largest in the world, handling parcels and personal written communications; cards and envelopes are first-class mail airlifted between stations, while book packets, registered newspapers and periodicals are second-class mail carried by surface mail. To facilitate quick delivery, six mail channels have been introduced: Rajdhani Channel, Metro Channel, Green Channel, Business Channel, Bulk Mail Channel and Periodical Channel. The telephone network with STD facility links the whole country at uniform rates. Mass communication provides entertainment and creates awareness through radio, television, newspapers, magazines, books and films; All India Radio (Akashwani) and Doordarshan broadcast a variety of programmes, newspapers are published in about 100 languages, and India is the largest producer of feature films in the world. Digital India is an umbrella programme to prepare India for a knowledge-based transformation.
topics.easyExplanation: Communication shares information through the post, telephone, radio, television, newspapers, films and the internet, and India has one of the largest networks in Asia.

topics.meaning: International trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries and is considered the economic barometer of a nation.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: The exchange of goods among people, states and countries is referred to as trade, and the market is the place where such exchanges take place. Trade between two countries is called international trade, which may take place through sea, air or land routes. While local trade is carried on in cities, towns and villages, and state-level trade between states, international trade reflects a country's economic prosperity and is considered the economic barometer for a country. Since resources are space bound, no country can survive without international trade. Export and import are the components of trade, and the balance of trade is the difference between a country's exports and imports; when exports exceed imports it is a favourable balance of trade, and when imports exceed exports it is an unfavourable balance of trade. India has trade relations with all major trading blocks and geographical regions of the world. Commodities exported from India include gems and jewellery, chemicals and related products, and agriculture and allied products, while imports include petroleum, machinery and electronic goods.
topics.easyExplanation: International trade is buying and selling goods between countries, and a country's trade shows how prosperous its economy is.

topics.meaning: Tourism is an important trade in India that promotes national integration, supports local handicrafts and cultural pursuits, and provides employment.
topics.ncertBasedExplanation: Tourism in India has grown substantially over the years and is recognised as an industry and an important trade. More than 15 million people are directly engaged in the tourism industry, and tourism also promotes national integration, provides support to local handicrafts and cultural pursuits, and helps in the development of international understanding about our culture and heritage. Foreign tourists visit India for heritage tourism, eco-tourism, adventure tourism, cultural tourism, medical tourism and business tourism, attracted by the country's rich cultural heritage, scenic beauty and historical monuments. Rajasthan, Goa, Jammu and Kashmir, and the temple towns of South India are important destinations for foreign tourists. Tourism earns foreign exchange and supports a large number of related activities and small businesses. There is a vast potential for the further development of tourism in almost all parts of the country, and efforts are being made to promote different types of tourism to increase its contribution to the economy.
